firebase-firestore.author: Yvonne Pan
firebase-firestore.message:
feat: add first, last, arrayAgg and arrayAggDistinct expressions (#7893)

Adds support for the accumulators pipeline expressions: `first`, `last`,
`arrayAgg` and `arrayAggDistinct`.

ported from https://github.com/firebase/firebase-js-sdk/pull/9576

firebase-firestore.author: Yvonne Pan
firebase-firestore.message:
feat: add arithmetic expressions (#7886)

Adds support for the arithmetic pipeline expression `trunc`.
The `rand` expression was already ported internally. Remove the internal
keyword and add an integration test for `rand` expression

ported from https://github.com/firebase/firebase-js-sdk/pull/9498

firebase-firestore.author: wu-hui
firebase-firestore.message:
Fix performance issue in ObjectValue.equals for large documents (#7884)

Revert to using Values.equals() instead of buildProto().equals() to
avoid exponential execution time on large/deeply nested documents. Fixes
#7877

firebase-firestore.author: cherylEnkidu
firebase-firestore.message:
Fixed a race condition in test and remove null / nan related operations (#7741)

## Description

This PR introduces the following Beta API changes and related fixes:

* **Removed API Methods:** Removes the `isNan`, `isNotNan`, `isNull`,
and `isNotNull` pipeline string/number operations.
* **Changed `alias` Return Type:** Updates the `alias` method to now
return an `AliasedExpression` instead of `Selectable`.
* **Changelog Update:** Updates `CHANGELOG.md` to reflect these recent
Beta API changes.
* **Test Fixes:** Fixes build failures in `QueryToPipelineTest.java`
caused by the race condition.

---------

Co-authored-by: gemini-code-assist[bot] <176961590+gemini-code-assist[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

firebase-installations.author: Vinay Guthal
firebase-installations.message:
Fix FIS ID duplication issue from backup data (#7096)

Currently, FIS apps are writing their data into the backup directory.
This causes an issue when a new installation retrieves data from the
backup, as it also picks up the previous FIS ID. This results in the FCM
server throwing an error, indicating that the FIS ID is already
associated with another instance. This PR fixes the issue for new apps
using FIS.
This PR also adds a function to the interop api which allows clearing of
fid cache. The implementation of the same is added in
FirebaseInstallations.java

Tested by creating two functions getDataFileBackup which stores it in
the backup directory and insertOrUpdatePersistedInstallationEntry which
the app data in the backup directory. So I call
insertOrUpdatePersistedInstallationEntry on the PersistedInstallation
object first and call getDataFileBackup and see that its back up
directory and then i call getDataFile() which would delete the
backupfile and return the nonbackupfile with that data. This was tested
by updating the PersistedInstallationTest.java . Also tested clearing
all this data with the clear function and it works

---------

Co-authored-by: Rodrigo Lazo <rlazo@users.noreply.github.com>

firebase-installations-interop.author: Vinay Guthal
firebase-installations-interop.message:
Fix FIS ID duplication issue from backup data (#7096)

Currently, FIS apps are writing their data into the backup directory.
This causes an issue when a new installation retrieves data from the
backup, as it also picks up the previous FIS ID. This results in the FCM
server throwing an error, indicating that the FIS ID is already
associated with another instance. This PR fixes the issue for new apps
using FIS.
This PR also adds a function to the interop api which allows clearing of
fid cache. The implementation of the same is added in
FirebaseInstallations.java

Tested by creating two functions getDataFileBackup which stores it in
the backup directory and insertOrUpdatePersistedInstallationEntry which
the app data in the backup directory. So I call
insertOrUpdatePersistedInstallationEntry on the PersistedInstallation
object first and call getDataFileBackup and see that its back up
directory and then i call getDataFile() which would delete the
backupfile and return the nonbackupfile with that data. This was tested
by updating the PersistedInstallationTest.java . Also tested clearing
all this data with the clear function and it works

---------

Co-authored-by: Rodrigo Lazo <rlazo@users.noreply.github.com>

ai-logic/firebase-ai.author: Andrew Heard
ai-logic/firebase-ai.message:
[AI] Use common `JSON` encoder in `LiveGenerativeModel` (#7880)

Switched from the default `kotlinx.serialization.json.Json` encoder to
`com.google.firebase.ai.common.JSON` in `LiveGenerativeModel`. `JSON`
sets [`explicitNulls =
false`](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-android-sdk/blob/537bb20b6034d52515eb52fb3acb494326902356/ai-logic/firebase-ai/src/main/kotlin/com/google/firebase/ai/common/APIController.kt#L93),
which should prevent `parameters` and `parametersJsonSchema` from both
being specified (one as `null`) in the serialized JSON.

---------

Co-authored-by: Rodrigo Lazo Paz <rlazo@google.com>

appcheck/firebase-appcheck.author: Rodrigo Lazo
appcheck/firebase-appcheck.message:
[Appcheck] Use default message if getToken exception doesn't include one  (#7640)

This addresses issue
https://github.com/firebase/firebase-android-sdk/issues/7624.
Previously, missing error messages in custom providers would the Task to
fail with an null argument exception. The app check contract states that
the task **will** succeed. In the case of error, the
`AppCheckTokenResult` object returned will include the error
information.

appcheck/firebase-appcheck-debug.author: Rodrigo Lazo
appcheck/firebase-appcheck-debug.message:
[Plugin] Fix build release artifacts workflow (#7693)

Downloading the dackka binary should happen **before** gradle is run to
build the artifacts, otherwise it doesn't find it and fails.

appcheck/firebase-appcheck-debug.author: Rodrigo Lazo
appcheck/firebase-appcheck-debug.message:

The change introduced in (#7640) triggers a release of app check, and all SDKs must contain a changelog entry for it.

transport/transport-api.author: Vinay Guthal
transport/transport-api.message:
add support for experimental ids (#7667)

This PR does the following
- Adds field experimentIdsEncryptedList to EventContext
- Adds field experimentIdsEncryptedList to EventInternal
- Updates Sqlite schema to include the above mentioned field
- Updates the ExperimentIds class to have setEncryptedBlob as a
list<string> (base 64 encoded of the byte array)
- Updates test proto to match the prod proto

transport/transport-runtime.author: Rodrigo Lazo
transport/transport-runtime.message:
[Infra] Fix `transport-runtime` test error (#7927)

Updated `TestService` to directly use `SynchronizationComponent` methods
(`getGuard`, `shutdown`) instead of
`DaggerSynchronizationComponent`. This simplifies the interaction with
the synchronization component and potentially reduces boilerplate
related to Dagger's generated classes in test contexts.

Standardized the `androidTestAnnotationProcessor` dependency in
`transport-runtime.gradle` to use `libs.dagger.compiler`, aligning it
with other Dagger dependency declarations.
